lesson_13.english_translation: "Wolf' (written by whites as Skidi or Skedee). Largest Pawnee band, separated from Southern Bands for many years; associated with the Arikaree.
"Little Mud Lodge Village." Or, perhaps "Little Swamp" from kit-kaharu ' , swamp, slough. Known in early literature as "Republican Pawnee," for the river where the villages were.

"Man Going East" or "Man Going Downstream." The first independent Southern Band, given birth by someone 's religious vision. Known as Tap-age (noisy) by the French on account of their "pentecostal" type of early morning prayers.
indicator for making verb phrase into noun

lesson_20_p117.skiri_form: Ti ratarak•akitah•ku Pari tuh•raru kskitiiks akitaru', he hiri we tahrak•ut; Tsawii, Kitkahahki, Pitahawirata, a Tskiri. Hiri wi•tuks•it•at akarraratau t'eks•kurit Kits Katus.
lesson_20_p117.english_translation: This our Pawnee tribe is made up of four bands; we are these: Tsawii, Kitkahahki, Pitahawirata and Tskiri. And it (the tribe) used to live, it is said, in mud lodges near the Platte ("Flat") River.
lesson_20_p117.full_sentence_pawnee: Ti ratarak•akitah•ku Pari tuh•raru kskitiiks akitaru', he hiri we tahrak•ut; Tsawii, Kitkahahki, Pitahawirata, a Tskiri. Hiri wi•tuks•it•at akarraratau t'eks•kurit Kits Katus.
lesson_20_p117.full_sentence_english: This our Pawnee tribe is made up of four bands; we are these: Tsawii, Kitkahahki, Pitahawirata and Tskiri. And it (the tribe) used to live, it is said, in mud lodges near the Platte ("Flat") River.

lesson_20_p117.skiri_form: Pitkus•ihiri asku ri•katiharu tirah•pares•pari ' . Wi•tiks-kawutik•usuku taraha a rahurahki. Hawa tikstarah•ka-wa•karuku rikisu', atit, pahuks. Tirarah•kiwi a wi•tiks-takas•karuk•usuku a tirah•tsaisiku atit a pahuks.
lesson_20_p117.english_translation: Twice a year they went hunting. It is said that they killed buffalo and deer, (game). In addition, they planted various things, corn, beans and squash. They would roast corn, and used to make jerky and they dried the beans and squash.
lesson_20_p117.full_sentence_pawnee: Pitkus•ihiri asku ri•katiharu tirah•pares•pari ' . Wi•tiks-kawutik•usuku taraha a rahurahki. Hawa tikstarah•ka-wa•karuku rikisu', atit, pahuks. Tirarah•kiwi a wi•tiks-takas•karuk•usuku a tirah•tsaisiku atit a pahuks.
lesson_20_p117.full_sentence_english: Twice a year they went hunting. It is said that they killed buffalo and deer, (game). In addition, they planted various things, corn, beans and squash. They would roast corn, and used to make jerky and they dried the beans and squash.

lesson_20_p117.skiri_form: Ku•kur•rutsiks•taru ruksiri•witau tsahriks raku•ka•wihat asku akarraratau. Hawa ah•rukstah•ka•wariki arusa pitsikat
lesson_20_p117.english_translation: Perhaps there were about thirty people dwelling in one mud lodge. Also they would put their horses inside in the winter.
lesson_20_p117.full_sentence_pawnee: Ku•kur•rutsiks•taru ruksiri•witau tsahriks raku•ka•wihat asku akarraratau. Hawa ah•rukstah•ka•wariki arusa pitsikat
lesson_20_p117.full_sentence_english: Perhaps there were about thirty people dwelling in one mud lodge. Also they would put their horses inside in the winter.

lesson_20_p117.english_translation: It is said that it (the tribe) had (many) customs which were very wonderful. The old ones were apparently sincere (about these things).
lesson_20_p117.full_sentence_english: It is said that it (the tribe) had (many) customs which were very wonderful. The old ones were apparently sincere (about these things).

lesson_20_p117.skiri_form: Wi•turuks•ka•hurar•ri hiri ah•ratukstak•kitkeu'. Tihe ra•akitarraruhat tsararat ra•hurit•at reh•kuhrah•witsa'. Resaru kuh•ri•ruhuru rakurak•tara hiri ra•saka•ku.
lesson_20_p117.english_translation: It must have been good land there where we lived. Those other enemy tribes about (us), attacked us all the time. The leaders, perhaps, decided to come down here to the south.
lesson_20_p117.full_sentence_pawnee: Wi•turuks•ka•hurar•ri hiri ah•ratukstak•kitkeu'. Tihe ra•akitarraruhat tsararat ra•hurit•at reh•kuhrah•witsa'. Resaru kuh•ri•ruhuru rakurak•tara hiri ra•saka•ku.
lesson_20_p117.full_sentence_english: It must have been good land there where we lived. Those other enemy tribes about (us), attacked us all the time. The leaders, perhaps, decided to come down here to the south.
